entities.name: Mihir
entities.context: Advisor who provides guidance on billing, invoicing, collections, and revenue structuring

entities.name: Vrijen
entities.context: Founder being advised; embraces founder-led sales philosophy

entities.name: Founder-Led Sales
entities.context: Sales approach where founders personally sell; hearing 'no' repeatedly is viewed as normal and necessary

entities.name: Over-Automation Risk
entities.context: Market theme: founders often over-invest in internal tools (like invoicing automation) before having enough customers to justify it

entities.name: Data Ownership as Differentiator
entities.context: VCs and employers increasingly care about owning talent data rather than renting access from intermediaries

entities.name: Acquisition Optics
entities.context: Quality and structure of revenue (annual, recurring, diversified) is at least as important as top-line numbers for acquirers

entities.name: Revenue Structuring
entities.context: Structuring revenue (monthly vs. annual) to look attractive for acquirers; guided by Mihir

entities.name: Stripe Invoicing
entities.context: Payment tool mentioned as example of unnecessary fees (take-rates) founders should avoid

entities.name: Manual Invoicing
entities.context: Acceptable early-stage approach before investing in billing systems; part of Mihir's guidance

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company; context for data ownership differentiator and talent intermediary dynamics

relationships.context: Provides guidance on billing, invoicing, collections, and revenue structuring
Counsels on structuring revenue (monthly vs. annual) to optimize for acquisition attractiveness
Warns about avoiding unnecessary fees like Stripe Invoicing take-rates
Guides on when manual invoicing is fine vs. when to invest in systems
Embraces founder-led sales; views hearing 'no' repeatedly as normal and necessary
Over-investing in invoicing automation before scale justifies it; manual is often sufficient early on
Employers wanting to own talent data rather than rent from intermediaries is relevant to Careerspan's positioning
How revenue is structured directly affects how acquirers evaluate a company
Recognized as a market theme relevant to early-stage founders like V
